steps.title: Use the given ratio
steps.content:
The problem gives 
$$
BC=EF=28
$$ and
$$
\frac{BC}{BA}=\frac{EF}{ED}
$$
Substitute the equal side lengths.
$$
\frac{28}{BA}=\frac{28}{ED}
$$
So $BA=ED$.

steps.title: Apply side angle side
steps.content: The triangles already have two pairs of equal sides, $BA=ED$ and $BC=EF$. The included angles at $B$ and $E$ are also equal because each measures $40^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Conclude no more is needed
steps.content: Those facts prove the triangles congruent by side-angle-side using only the information already given. The answer is D.
